properties.remark: ROCKS AND MUD WERE WASHED ONTO BADWATER ROAD. THE ROAD WAS CLOSED FROM 6 AM UNTIL NOON.
MUD AND ROCKS RENDERED HIGHWAY 190 IMPASSABLE AT TOWNES PASS.
A SPOTTER REPORTED PLUMES OF BLOWING DUST FROM THE ELDORADO DRY LAKE BED WERE REDUCING VISIBILITY TO LESS THAN 100 FEET AT TIMES ON HIGHWAY 95.
A 61 MPH WIND GUST FROM THE WEST SOUTHWEST WAS MEASURED BY THE SIERRA ROTORS 2 SENSOR AT AN ELEVATION OF 4842 FEET.
AN EMPLOYEE AT THE SOLAR ONE POWER PLANT ABOUT 15 MILES SOUTHWEST OF BOULDER CITY REPORTED HAIL THAT WAS ESTIMATED TO BE THE SIZE OF QUARTERS.
A SPOTTER REPORTED A SIX FOOT SWATH OF WATER RUNNING ACROSS A ROAD IN MEADVIEW.
THREE DOCKS WERE CAPSIZED OR SEPERATED AT THE CALLVILLE BAY MARINA BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
LAS VEGAS FIRE DEPARTMENT COMMUNICATIONS CENTER REPORTED SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES WERE BLOWN OVER.
LAS VEGAS FIRE DEPARTMENT COMMUNICATIONS CENTER RELAYED A REPORT OF SEVERAL SWIFTWATER RESCUES IN THE AREA OF WARM SPRINGS AND PECOS.
HENDERSON EXECUTIVE AIRPORT RECORDED A 62 MPH THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST.
PUBLIC REPORT OF SEVERAL SMALL TREES DOWN AT LAS VEGAS BOULEVARD AND PEBBLE ROAD IN SOUTH LAS VEGAS. WIND GUSTS OF 45 MPH WERE ALSO ESTIMATED.
A SPOTTER REPORTED SIGNIFICANT FLOODING AT EASTERN AND WARM SPRINGS WITH ESTIMATES OF AT LEAST 3 FEET OF WATER ON THE ROADS AND SEVERAL CARS ABANDONED.
LAS VEGAS AREA BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTD THAT A TREE FELL ON A CAR AND DAMAGED IT NEAR RUSSELL ROAD. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT IS ESTIMATED.
NUMEROUS TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN THROUGHOUT THE NORTH SIDE AND EAST SIDE OF THE LAS VEGAS VALLEY.
THE PUBLIC THROUGH LAS VEGAS AREA BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED THAT A FIREWORKS STAND WAS BLOWN OVER BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS. ABOUT HALF OF THE SUPPLY OF FIREWORKS WAS DAMAGED.
A SPOTTER REPORTED ONE POWER POLE DOWN AND ONE BENT BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS NEAR CHARLESTON AND NELLIS. THE TIME OF THIS EVENT WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
NORTH LAS VEGAS AIRPORT MEASURED A 59 MPH THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST.
A SPOTTER REPORTED POWER POLES DOWN ALONG WITH POWER LINES AS WELL AS SEVERAL DOWNED TREES NEAR JONES BOULEVARD AND LAKE MEAD BOULEVARD AND NEAR RANCHO AND VEGAS DRIVE.

*** 1 INJ *** LAS VEGAS AREA BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED ONE MAN WAS RESCUED BY THE CLARK COUNTY FIRE DEPARTMENT AT FLAMINGO AND SWENSON FROM A PERSON TRAPPED IN A FLOODED WASH. THE MAN SUFFERED MINOR INJURIES.
NEVADA ENERGY REPORTED NEARLY 10,000 RESIDENTS LOST POWER THROUGHOUT THE LAS VEGAS VALLEY DUE TO THUNDERSTORMS.
A SPOTTER REPORTED POWER OUT NORTH OF CHARLESTON BOULEVARD ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE LAS VEGAS VALLEY. IN ADDITION...8 POWER POLES AND 20 LIGHT POLES WERE KNOCKED DOWN BETWEEN NELLIS AND CHRISTY.
MUD WAS REPORTED OVER HIGHWAY 375.
TWO TO THREE FEET OF WATER WAS FLOWING ACROSS STATE ROUTE 190 NEAR EMIGRANT PASS. THE ROADWAY WAS SHUT DOWN AS A RESULT.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED SCOTTYS CASTLE ROAD WAS COVERED WITH LIGHT DEBRIS. UBEHEBE ROAD WAS COVERED WITH OVER A FOOT OF DEBRIS.
SPOTTER REPORTS 6 TO 12 INCHES OF WATER ACROSS HIGHWAY 62 IN TWENTYNINE PALMS.
SPOTTER REPORTS DAMAGE TO AWNING IN JOSHUA TREE FROM TSTM WIND GUST AT 215 PM.
NEWSPAPER REPORTS ROADS WASHED OUT IN SPRING MOUNTAIN RANCH STATE PARK DUE TO FLASH FLOODING
BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTS SEVERAL AREAS OF FLASH FLOODING NEAR HIGHWAY 160 AT BONNIE SPRINGS. VIDEOS SHOW SEVERAL FEET OF WATER RUSHING IN PLACES. ARROYO ROAD AT BLUE DIAMOND WAS WASHED OUT.
STATE ROUTE 168 WAS COVERED WITH MUD...SAND AND GRAVEL BETWEEN THE NORTH LAKE TURNOFF AND LAKE SABRINA. THE EASTBOUND LANE WAS COVERED WITH MUD AFTER A MUDSLIDE TOOK PLACE.

A SPOTTER REPORTED IN NEEDLES THAT POWER WAS OUT AND TRANSFORMERS WERE KNOCKED OUT BY THE WIND. TREE BRANCHES WERE ALSO DOWN AND SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF ROOFS. IN ADDITION THE AWNING OF A GAS STATION WAS SWAYING IN THE WIND. VISIBILITY WAS REDUCED TO LESS THAN 5 BLOCKS.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS UPROOTED TWO LARGE MESQUITE TREES IN ROTARY COMMUNITY PARK IN LAKE HAVASU CITY. IN ADDITION NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS WERE REPORTED DOWN AND ROUGH LAKE WATERS CAUSED SOME BEACH EROSION.
THE LAKE HAVASU CITY POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED WATER UP TO THE BOTTOM OF CARS ON ROADS THAT WAS AT LEAST 6 INCHES DEEP. IN ADDITION ONE CAR WAS STALLED IN FLOODWATERS.
PER CHP WEB PAGE ROADWAY FLOODING WAS REPORTED ON STATE ROUTE 190 JUST EAST OF GUNSITE.
PER CHP WEB SITE THE DANTES VIEW ROAD JUST EAST OF DANTES VIEW WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
PER CHP STATE ROUTE 190 CLOSED AT 395 DUE TO FLOODING.
CHP REPORTED STATE ROUTE 190 WAS CLOSED NEAR DARWIN DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
CHP REPORTS THAT STATE ROUTE 190 WAS CLOSED FROM HIGHWAY 395 ON THE WEST TO ROUTE 127 ON THE EAST DUE TO FLOODING NEAR 395 AND AROUND THE DANTES VIEW TURN OFF.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED 20 MULE CANYON ROAD WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. THE START TIME OF THIS EVENT IS AN ESTIMATE.

NEWSPAPER PHOTOGRAPHS SHOWED AND CHP REPORTED WATER AND MUD ACROSS TRONA ROAD NEAR SEARLES STATION ROAD. WATER AND MUD WERE REPORTED ON THE ROAD AS LATE AS 933 PM. THE START TIME OF THIS EVENT IS AN ESTIMATE BASED OFF OF RADAR AND THE TRONA CO-OP OBSERVER REPORT.
SPOTTER REPORTS VERY STRONG TSTM WIND GUST AT 301 PM. CARS WERE SWAYED ON THE ROADWAY. ESTIMATED 60 MPH.
SPOTTER REPORTS SIX INCHES OF WATER AND ROCKS ACROSS HIGHWAY 93 AT SEVERAL PLACES IN BOULDER CITY.
CHP REPORTED POLE LINE ROAD FROM MELDORA TO SUNRISE FLOODED AND CLOSED. THEY ALSO REPORTED LOTS OF DEBRIS AND A SWEEPER WILL BE NEEDED FOR COUNTY ROADS.
MUD AND DEBRIS ACROSS THE ROADWAY AT PACIFICA IN BOULDER CITY.
MUD AND ROCKS ACROSS HIGHWAY 95 AT MILE MARKER 46.
CHP REPORTS FLOODING ON STATE ROUTE 190 JUST WEST OF PANAMINT VALLEY ROAD. UNKNOWN NUMBER OF PEOPLE OUT OF THEIR VEHICLES STANDING ON HIGH GROUND.
CHP REPORTED PANAMINT VALLEY ROAD JUST SOUTH OF STATE ROUTE 190 COMPLETELY WASHED OUT AND WATER FLOWING HEAVILY BUT NO VEHICLES STUCK.
THE DOLAN SPRINGS FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED MUD AND ROCKS WASHED ONTO PIERCE FERRY ROAD NEAR DOLAN SPRINGS. FLOWING WATER ALSO CROSSED THE ROAD. THE FLOODING ENDED AROUND 730 PM SATURDAY.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS RESULTED IN DOCKS BREAKING LOOSE AND FUEL LINES BEING PULLED APART AT THE TEMPLE BAR MARINA. THE MARINA WAS CLOSED SUNDAY AS A RESULT.
A 60 FOOT TALL TREE WAS UPROOTED BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW A TREE ONTO A HOUSE ON VEGAS DRIVE.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW A TREE ONTO A HOUSE ON VEGAS DRIVE NEAR SIMMONS STREET. THE TREE LANDED IN THE LIVING ROOM OF THE HOUSE.
2 LARGE SIGNS...A WENDYS AND A WALRGREES SIGN...WERE BLOWN OUT AND ONTO THE GROUND AT THE INTERSECTION OF RANCHO AND WASHINGTON. THE TIME IS ESTIMATED.
LARGE TREE SNAPPED AT THE BASE IN THE RANCHO CIRCLE AREA. THE EVENT TIME WAS ESTIMATED.
AN ENTIRE LINE OF POWER POLES WAS SNAPPED OFF...AT LEAST A BLOCK LONG...AND POWER WAS OUT ALONG CHEYENNE AVENUE FROM VALLEY DRIVE TO ALLEN DRIVE. TIME IS ESTIMATED.
AN ENTIRE LINE OF POWER POLES WAS SNAPPED OFF...AT LEAST A BLOCK LONG...AND POWER WAS OUT. TIME IS ESTIMATED.

*** 1 INJ *** LAS VEGAS FIRE PUBLIC INFORMATION OFFICER AND MEDIA REPORTED THAT THE 80 PERCENT OF THE ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF TWO BUILDINGS AT AN APARTMENT COMPLEX AND THE MATERIALS WERE BLOWN INTO ONE BUILDING AT THE COMPLEX BREAKING TWO WINDOWS AND DAMAGING 4 DOORS. ONE CHILD SUFFERED MINOR INJURIES FROM FLYING DEBRIS AND WAS TREATED ON SCENE AND RELEASED. AN AIR CONDITIONING UNIT WAS ALSO MOVED. MORE THAN 100 FAMILIES AT THE COMPLEX WERE DISPLACED BY THE DAMAGE.

*** 1 INJ *** LAS VEGAS FIRE PUBLIC INFORMATION OFFICER RELAYED A REPORT OF WIRES DOWN IN THE AREA OF THE NORTH LAS VEGAS AIRPORT. IN ADDITION...ROOFING MATERIALS WERE BLOWN OFF OF A BUILDING AND INTO THE APARTMENT NEXT DOOR BREAKING TWO WINDOWS AND DAMAGING 4 DOORS. ONE CHILD SUFFERED MINOR INJURIES FROM FLYING DEBRIS AND WAS TREATED ON SCENE AND RELEASED.

*** 1 INJ *** LAS VEGAS FIRE PUBLIC INFORMATION OFFICER RELAYED A REPORT OF WIRES DOWN IN THE AREA OF THE NORTH LAS VEGAS AIRPORT. IN ADDITION...ROOFING MATERIALS WERE BLOWN OFF OF A BUILDING AND INTO THE APARTMENT NEXT DOOR BREAKING TWO WINDOWS AND DAMAGING 4 DOORS. ONE CHILD SUFFERED MINOR INJURIES FROM FLYING DEBRIS AND WAS TREATED ON SCENE AND RELEASED.

THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED THAT 20 VEHICLES PARKED AT THE LAS VEGAS BOAT HARBOR HAD HAIL OF AN UNKNOWN SIZE AND DEBRIS SUCH AS ROCKS AND MUD HIT THEM AND BREAK OUT THEIR BACK WINDOWS. IN ADDITION TWO DOCKS BROKE APART AT THE LAS VEGAS BOAT HARBOR INCLUDING ONE OF THE MAIN HEADWALKS. THIS EVENT TIME ESTIMATED OFFICE OF RADAR.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS DOWNED A POWER LINE IN LAKE MEAD CITY NEAR SMITH ROAD AND RAINBOW ROAD.
WATER RISING INTO HOMES...SEVERAL ROADWAYS CLOSED. WATER FLOWING FROM MOUNTAINS NORTHEAST OF TOWN.

RUNOFF DUE TO HEAVY RAIN AND SNOW RESULTED IN MUD AND WATER RUSHING DOWN THE MOUNT WHITNEY TRAIL AND INTO THE WHITNEY PORTAL CAMPGROUND. THE WATER AND MUD WAS REPORTED TO BE WAIST DEEP IN SOME AREAS. ONE WOODEN BRIDGE OVER CREEK NEAR THE CAMPGROUND WAS OVERTOPPED BY THE WATER. THE CAMPGROUND WAS CLOSED. HELICOPTER SEARCH AND RESCUE OPERATIONS WERE CONDUCTED...WITH ONE PERSON RESCUED AT 715 AM ON 7/30 DUE TO SEVERE HYPOTHERMIA FROM THE WHITNEY TRAIL. AS MANY AS 25 TO 30 DAY HIKERS WERE STRANDED.
WATER AND GRAVEL ON STATE ROUTE 190.

CHP TRAFFIC INCIDENT INFORMATION PAGE SHOWS ROADWAY FLOODING ON WHITNEY PORTAL ROAD WEST OF LONE PINE. ROADWAY CLOSED 3 MILES WEST OF LONE PINE. CALL INTO INYO COUNTY SHERIFF...0842 PM PDT...PORTIONS OF WHITNEY PORTAL ROAD IS WASHED OUT. CAMPERS TRAPPED IN THE CAMPGROUNDS WEST OF LONE PINE. FLOODING TAKING PLACE IN LONE PINE WITH WATER OVER PORTIONS OF HIGHWAY 395.
STATE ROUTE 62 WAS REPORTED FLOODED WITH AROUND SIX INCHES OF WATER NEAR WHITE FEATHER STREET.
A TRUCK STOP EMPLOYEE AT KRAMAR JUNCTION ESTIMATED THUNDERSTORM WINDS OF AT LEAST 60 MPH.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED SALINE VALLEY ROAD WAS WASHED OUT AND CLOSED IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK. ROCKS AND MUD WERE ALSO WASHED ONTO BADWATER ROAD.

THE ESMERALDA COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED THAT ROCKS...MUD AND A LARGE AMOUNT OF WATER WERE ON STATE ROUTE 266 NEAR LIDA. A SINGLE VEHICLE ROLLOVER OCCURRED IN THIS AREA AS WELL...BUT THERE WERE NO INJURIES REPORTED.
THE MOHAVE COUNTY SHERIFF RELAYED A REPORT FROM A RANCHER OF AROUND SIX INCHES OF WATER NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF STOCKTON HILL ROAD AND CALLE MARQUEZ ROAD.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED THAT STATE ROUTE 136 WAS CLOSED DUE TO A FLASH FLOOD. ABOUT 20 VEHICLES WERE TRAPPED ON HIGHWAY 190 DUE TO THE VOLUME OF WATER ON THE ROADS.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED POWER LINES WERE DOWN AT STATE ROUTE 58 AT IRWIN ROAD.
SPOTTER REPORTED A MICROBURST NEAR AN AREA OF VIRGA. MEASURED A 64 MPH WIND GUST AT 1138 AM. WIND WAS STILL GUSTING TO 48 MPH A FEW MINUTES LATER.
5 POWER POLES WERE KNOCKED DOWN BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS ALONG COMMUNITY BOULEVARD BETWEEN SUMMERSET ROAD AND DIXIE ROAD.
A SPOTTER REPORTED DAMAGE TO A SHED AND AN AWNING ON THEIR PROPERTY AS WELL AS LOOSE ITEMS BLOWN AROUND. A WIND GUST TO 64 MPH WAS ALSO MEASURED. THE THUNDERSTORM LASTED APPROXIMATELY 20 MINUTES.
DAMAGE TO SHED AND AWNING ON PROPERTY. LOOSE ITEMS BLOWN AROUND. ALSO HAD A GUST TO 64 MPH...LASTED APPROXIMATELY 20 MINUTES. DELAYED REPORT.
AROUND 6 INCHES OF WATER WAS REPORTED NEAR AMBOY ROAD AND LELIA LANE BY THE CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED DEBRIS ON SCOTTYS CASTLE ROAD DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED MUD AND ROCKS WERE WASHED ONTO HIGHWAY 190 NEAR COW CREEK BY FLOODWATERS.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW OVER A BLOCK WALL AT A RESIDENCE IN HAVASU HEIGHTS CAUSING DAMAGE TO THE EXTERIOR OF A SUNROOM AT THE RESIDENCE.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW OVER A CAMPER ON INTERSTATE 40 NEAR MILE MARKER 13.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED A LOT OF FLOODING ON MORNING STAR MINE ROAD WITH MUD AND ROCKS ON THE ROAD.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED A LOT OF FLOODING ON MORNING STORE MINE ROAD WITH MUD AND ROCKS ON THE ROAD.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A WIRE DOWN AND SPARKING ON MORNINGSIDE ROAD AT SUNSET ROAD.
FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED A ROOF AND A PORCH OFF OF A MOBILE HOME. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE OFF OF RADAR.
FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED THAT DEBRIS HAD FLOWED ACROSS HIGHWAY 62 IN JOSHUA TREE.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A TREE DOWN BLOCKING THE NORTHBOUND LANES OF SUNSET AND OLEANDER IN JOSHUA TREE. A REPORT OF A SHED IN THE ROADWAY WITH METAL DEBRIS AND NAILS ON THE ROAD NEAR SUNSET ROAD AND STATE ROUTE 62 WAS ALSO RECEIVED.

THUNDERSTORM WINDS STRIPPED ROOFS AND SIDING OF ALUMINUM SHEDS...BLEW OF SECTIONS OF THE JOSHUA TREE LIBRARY ROOF...BLEW OVER A LANDMARK 36 FOOT WIDE BILLBOARD SIGN...TIPPED OVER A BUILDING HOUSING A CROCHET MUSEUM AND DOWNED SEVERAL TREES...SOME OF WHICH FELL ONTO BUILDINGS. PARTS OF JOSHUA TREE LOST POWER FOR SEVERAL HOURS. EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A WIRE DOWN ON HIGHWAY 62.
MUD AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS RECHE ROAD JUST WEST OF NORTH BORDER AVENUE. ROAD IS PASSABLE.
TWO SEPERATE REPORTS OF HAIL AT LEAST QUARTER SIZE IN BAKER...ONE FROM IHOP AND THE OTHER FROM A CONVENIENCE STORE. HAIL COVERED THE GROUND AND WAS STILL THERE TWENTY MINUTES LATER.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED IRWIN ROAD JUST WEST OF FORT IRWIN ROAD HAS A FEW SPOTS WERE AT LEAST 6 INCHES OF WATER COVERED THE ROAD AND THE ROAD WAS NOT PASSABLE.
LAS VEGAS AREA BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER FLOODED THE CIRCUS CIRCUS ADVENTUREDOME.
LAS VEGAS AREA BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED A HOME ON THE 15TH STREET BETWEEN FREMONET AND CHARLESTON HAD MORE THAN A FOOT OF WATER INSIDE OF IT.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL NEAR CRAIG AND INTERSTATE 15. EVENT TIME BASED ON RADAR
QUARTER SIZE HAIL NEAR CRAIG AND INTERSTATE 15. THE EVENT TIME WAS BASED ON RADAR.
A SPOTTER ESTIMATED 60 MPH WINDS NEAR NELLIS AFB.
SPOTTER ESTIMATES 60 MPH WINDS NEAR NELLIS AFB.
LAS VEGAS BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED A CAR WAS STRANDED IN FLOODWATERS UP TO WINDOWS NEAR ALEXANDER AND LOSEE.

LAS VEGAS AREA BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED THE PARKING LOT AT THE CANNERY CASINO IN NORTH LAS VEGAS FLOODED...WITH SOME CARS PARTIALLY UNDER WATER. A FEW INCHES OF WATER ALSO ENTERED PART OF THE CASINO AND MOVIE THEATER.
ESTIMATED DIME TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL ALONG HIGHWAY 93 NEAR WIKIEUP
WIND ESTIMATED AT 50 MPH DOWNED A FEW POWER LINES IN DOWNTOWN LAS VEGAS KNOCKING OUT POWER. A FEW NEIGHBORHOODS ALONG DECATUR BOULVEARD NEAR ALTA...VALLEY VIEW AND CHARLESTON ALSO LOST POWER.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A FOOT OF WATER OVER HIGHWAY 95 NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 68.
0.90 INCH OF RAIN IN AN HOUR OR LESS. MUD SLID OVER SOME ROADS. THE EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED.

A WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED THAT 12 TO 14 INCHES OF WATER WAS FLOWING ACROSS THE INTERSECTION OF NELLIS BOULEVARD AND STEWART AVENUE. BOTH ROADS WERE COVERED WITH WATER A QUARTER-MILE IN EACH DIRECTION.
WATER WAS REPORTED FLOWING ACROSS THE INTERSECTION OF BOULDER HIGHWAY AND RUSSEL ROAD AND SPILLING OVER INTO A NEARBY FLOOD CHANNEL.
A SWIFT WATER RESCUE WAS REPORTED BY THE CLARK COUNTY FIRE DEPARTMENT AT THE INTERSECTION OF CHEYENNE AVENUE AND LAS VEGAS BOULEVARD.

FLOODWATERS BUILT UP BEHIND A BACKYARD WALL AT A HOME IN HENDERSON NEAR HORIZON RIDGE AND MISSION AND STRAINED THE WALL TO THE POINT THAT IT BROKE. A FOUR FOOT WALL OF WATER AND MUD RUSHED INTO THE YARD OF A HOME...FLOODING MOST OF THE FIRST FLOOR AND INUNDATING A SWIMMING POOL. MUD AND DEBRIS WASHED ONTO NEARBY PANGLOSS STREET.
ROCKS...DEBRIS AND MUD WERE ON SOME ROADS NEAR US 95 AND WAGON WHEEL DRIVE. ROAD CREWS HAD TO USE A BULLDOZER TO REMOVE IT FROM THE ROADS.
ROCKS...DEBRIS AND MUD WERE ON SOME ROADS NEAR US 95 AND WAGON WHEEL DRIVE. ROAD CREWS HAD TO USE A BULLDOZER TO REMOVE TO REMOVE IT FROM THE ROADS.
CHP REPORTED A VEHICLE STUCK IN THE MUD AT HIGHWAY 62 AND IRONAGE ROAD.
CHP REPORTED ROAD CLOSED AT HIGHWAY 62 MILE MARKER 58 DUE TO MUD AND WATER OVER THE ROAD. A BIG RIG IS STUCK IN THE MUD.
CHP REPORTED ROAD CLOSED AT HIGHWAY 62 MILE MARKER 58 WITH MUD ADN WATER OVER THE ROAD. A BIG RIG IS STUCK IN THE MUD.
CHP REPORTED ROAD CLOSED ON HIGHWAY 62 NEAR MILE MARKER 58 DUE TO MUD AND WATER ON THE ROADWAY. IT WAS ALSO REPORTED THAT A BIG RIG WAS STUCK IN THE MUD.
CHP REPORTED THE ROAD CLOSED ON HIGHWAY 62 NEAR MILE MARKER 58 DUE TO WATER AND MUD ACROSS THE ROAD. IT WAS ALSO REPORTED THAT A BIG RIG WAS STUCK IN THE MUD.
HORIZON RIDGE PARKWAY AND GIBSON ROAD EASTBOUND CLOSED DUE TO A ROCK OVER THE ROAD AT WHEELER.
HORIZON RIDGE PARKWAY AND GIBSON ROAD WESTBOUND CLOSED DUE TO A ROCK OVER THE ROAD AT WHEELER.
SWIFT WATER RESUES AT THE JUNCTION OF FLAMINGO RD AND I-215. WATER UP TO CAR WINDOWS.
BOULDER CITY POLICE REPORTS NUMEROUS CITY STREETS WITH MORE THAN SIX INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS THEM. THE WORST RUNOFF IS OCCURING AT THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 93 AT THE BUCHANAN INTERSECTION.
COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED DEBRIS OVER THE ROAD ON SR 322 NEAR MILE MARKER 10. IN ADDITION SMALL HAIL COVERED THE ROAD.
THE COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED SIGNIFICANT WATER OVER THE ROADWAY ON SR 322 NEAR MILE MARKER 10. DEPTH UKNOWN.

LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED 3 TO 4 INCHES OF MUD AND WATER RUNNING THROUGH THE CATHEDRAL GORGE STATE PARK CAMPGROUND. THE WATER AND MUD CHASED ABOUT 20 PEOPLE WHO WERE CAMPING OUT OF THEIR TENTS. THE WATER AND MUD FLOW WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 75 FEET ACROSS. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED 2 TO 3 INCHES OF WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWING OVER U.S. 93 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 108 AND 109. THE ROAD WAS STILL PASSABLE AT THE TIME.
DRY MICROBURST PRODUCED A 59 MPH WIND GUST AT THE BISHOP AIRPORT.
THIS GUST CAME FROM A DISSIPATING THUNDERSTORM.
THIS GUST CAME FROM A DISSIPATING THUNDERSTORM.
THIS GUST WAS PRODUCED BY THE SAME DISSIPATING THUNDERSTORM WHICH PRODUCED THE OTHER TWO HIGH WIND GUSTS ON THE SOUTHERN PORTION OF THE NEVADA NATIONAL SECURITY SITE.
A MESONET SITE ON THE NEVADA NATIONAL SECURITY SITE RECORDED A 63 MPH PEAK GUST.
WATER AND DEBRIS WERE FLOWING ACROSS TEMPLE BAR ROAD BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 14 AND 19.5.

SEVERAL AREA RAIN GAUGES REPORTED AROUND AN INCH OF RAIN BETWEEN 1130AM AND 1230PM IN THE VICINITY OF JIMMY DURANTE BLVD AND TROPICANA AVE. SEVERAL CARS BECAME STRANDED IN THE INTERSECTION DUE TO THE HIGH WATER AND A CLARK COUNTY SWIFT WATER RESCUE TEAM HELPED DRIVERS FROM THEIR CARS. BROADCAST MEDIA STATED THAT THE WATER FLOODED THE ROAD SO FAST THAT THE DRIVERS WERE NOT EXPECTING IT AND THEIR CARS STALLED. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
A TRAINED SPOTTER BETWEEN RUSSEL AND SUNSET ON STEPHANIE REPORTED WATER FLOWING OVER AND ABOVE THE CURB AND COVERING THREE QUATERS OF THE ROADWAY.
A TRAINED SPOTTER ON MARKS STREET REPORTED WATER AS DEEP AS 18 INCHES ON THE ROADWAY BETWEEN AMERICAN PACIFIC AND WARM SPRINGS.

*** 2 INJ *** HIGH WINDS TRIGGERED A DUST STORM WHICH REDUCED VISIBILITY TO NEAR ZERO ALONG A STRETCH OF HIGHWAY 395 NEAR BLACKROCK. CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A TOTAL OF 7 VEHICLES WERE INVOLVED IN 4 SEPERATE VEHICLE CRASHES. A CHP PATROL CAR RESPONDING TO THE CRASH WAS HIT TWICE AND TOTALED. 2 PEOPLE WERE HOSPITALIZED WITH INJURIES. ONE PERSON SUFFERED MAJOR INJURIES WITH A BROKEN FOOT. ANOTHER PERSON HAD THEIR HEAD GO THROUGH A WINDOW ON THEIR CAR.
